Title: Headgroup biosynthesis of phosphatidylcholine and phosphatidylethanolamine in seed plants

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Dephosphorylation of phosphoethanolamine and phosphocholine influences ethanolamine/choline pools during phosphate starvation, but has little effect on bulk membrane phospholipid levels.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant protein phosphatase assay; T-DNA knockout; CRISPR; overexpression; metabolite profiling; GUS/Venus reporter localization
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by phosphate starvation; expressed in hypocotyl, leaf veins, anthers
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by phosphate starvation; expressed in hypocotyl, leaf veins, anthers
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PECP1 encodes a Mg2+-dependent phosphatase with higher catalytic efficiency toward phosphoethanolamine than phosphocholine. 2. PECP1 is induced by phosphate starvation and localizes to the ER. 3. In vivo, PECP1 contributes mainly to PEtn dephosphorylation and acts redundantly with PS2 in phosphobase homeostasis under phosphate starvation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review indicates PECP1 likely functions more in phosphobase turnover/remodeling than in de novo phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with PECP1 in phosphocholine/phosphoethanolamine homeostasis during phosphate starvation, but is not established as the main phosphobase phosphatase in vitro.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; double-mutant analysis; overexpression; co-expression analysis; reporter localization; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by phosphate starvation; expressed in hypocotyls, leaf veins, anther filaments
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by phosphate starvation; expressed in hypocotyls, leaf veins, anther filaments
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PS2 is strongly associated with phosphate starvation responses and co-expressed with membrane lipid remodeling genes. 2. Although recombinant PS2 did not show clear PCho phosphatase activity in vitro, genetics support partial redundancy with PECP1 in vivo. 3. PS2 overexpression decreased PCho and PEtn levels, but its primary biochemical substrate remains uncertain.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also called PPsase1; root hair phenotypes under phosphate starvation were reported inconsistently across studies.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethanolamine production and thereby supports phosphatidylethanolamine and phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis; loss causes embryonic lethality.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: E. coli enzyme assay; yeast complementation; EMS/T-DNA knockout; genetic complementation; overexpression; metabolite profiling; Venus localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hypocotyls, leaf veins, root vasculature, stems, anthers, pollen grains, anther filaments; during seed development in endothelium to endosperm and embryos
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Hypocotyls, leaf veins, root vasculature, stems, anthers, pollen grains, anther filaments; during seed development in endothelium to endosperm and embryos
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: short-day condition; heavy metal ions Ni2+ or Mn2+ induce transcript
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SDC1 converts serine to ethanolamine and is required for Etn, PtdEtn and PtdCho production in Arabidopsis. 2. sdc1 null mutants are embryonic lethal, while weaker mutants show developmental defects rescued by ethanolamine. 3. Overexpression elevates Etn, PtdCho and PtdEtn and perturbs amino acid pools, indicating metabolic crosstalk.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to increase phospholipid headgroup precursor supply, but strong perturbation risks embryo lethality and amino-acid metabolic imbalance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Embryonic lethality when knocked out; altered glycine, glutamine, aspartate and threonine pools upon overexpression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review highlights unusual mitochondrial localization and possible competition with amino acid metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ATP-dependent phosphorylation of choline to phosphocholine in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions mainly as a choline kinase in vivo, affecting phosphocholine/ choline balance and ER stress response more than bulk phospholipid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; radiolabel pulse-chase; T-DNA knockout; complementation; overexpression; metabolite profiling; GUS/Venus localization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CEK1 is a major in vivo choline kinase in Arabidopsis. 2. cek1 mutants accumulate choline, reduce phosphocholine, and are hypersensitive to tunicamycin. 3. CEK1 has little detectable effect on total phospholipid composition under normal conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review concludes CEK1, CEK2 and CEK3 are Cho kinases, whereas CEK4 is the only in vivo Etn kinase.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor choline kinase contributing to phosphocholine production; knockout causes short roots but weak biochemical effect relative to CEK1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; T-DNA knockout; GUS/Venus localization; enzyme activity analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hypocotyls, leaf vasculature, inflorescence stems, roots, stigma
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Hypocotyls, leaf vasculature, inflorescence stems, roots, stigma
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CEK2 behaves as a choline kinase in vivo. 2. Its knockout has weaker effects on choline kinase activity than CEK1 but shows root shortening. 3. CEK2 is ER-localized and expressed in vascular/root tissues and stigma.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No strong effect on bulk membrane lipid content reported.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Specifically phosphorylates choline in vitro and functions as a choline kinase in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports phosphocholine production in roots; knockout causes short-root and root apical meristem defects that can be rescued by CEK3 or exogenous phosphocholine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; T-DNA knockout; complementation; phosphocholine supplementation; GUS/Venus localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hypocotyls, leaf vasculature, inflorescence stems, roots, young stamens
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Hypocotyls, leaf vasculature, inflorescence stems, roots, young stamens
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CEK3 is a choline-specific kinase among Arabidopsis CEKs. 2. cek3 mutants show root growth and meristem defects. 3. The phenotype is rescued by genomic CEK3 and by phosphocholine, linking CEK3 to PCho supply.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Biochemical impact on overall Cho kinase activity is weaker than CEK1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ATP-dependent phosphorylation of ethanolamine to phosphoethanolamine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The sole in vivo ethanolamine kinase in Arabidopsis; required for PtdEtn/PtdCho biosynthesis and embryogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; T-DNA knockout; complementation; overexpression; lipid profiling; GUS/Venus localization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CEK4 is the only ethanolamine kinase functioning in vivo in Arabidopsis. 2. cek4 knockout is embryonic lethal, showing its essentiality. 3. CEK4 overexpression increases membrane lipids including PtdCho and PtdEtn.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible engineering target to boost phospholipid synthesis, but essentiality and embryo lethality require cautious modulation rather than knockout.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review contrasts critical CEK4 function with more dispensable Cho kinases CEK1-3.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Sequentially methylates phosphoethanolamine and its mono-/dimethyl intermediates to generate phosphocholine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major determinant of phosphocholine supply for phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis; essential with PMT2/PMT3 for de novo PtdCho formation and plant viability.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast complementation; recombinant enzyme kinetics; T-DNA mutants; double/triple mutant analysis; promoter-swap complementation; metabolite profiling; GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant phospho-base N-methyltransferase with tandem methyltransferase domains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root tips and vascular tissues of rosette leaves; inflorescence stem; septum of pistils
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root tips and vascular tissues of rosette leaves; inflorescence stem; septum of pistils
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: salinity; microbial infection; phosphocholine/choline-responsive uORF-mediated translational repression reported for PMT1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PMT1 catalyzes phosphobase methylation toward phosphocholine and is a major control point in PtdCho biosynthesis. 2. Single pmt1 mutants show short-root defects, while combined loss with PMT3 or PMT2 intensifies growth defects and triple loss abolishes de novo PtdCho biosynthesis. 3. PMTs collectively define the sole route to phosphocholine/PtdCho production in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering phosphatidylcholine and stress adaptation, but gene dosage and isoform expression must be balanced to avoid severe root and viability defects.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Short-root phenotype, meristem defects, dwarfism in combinations, seedling lethality in triple mutants; antisense suppression caused pleiotropic defects and salt hypersensitivity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also known as XPL1/PEAMT/NMT1/DPR2; review explicitly states PMTs are the bottleneck in PtdCho biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates phosphobase intermediates in the PEtn-to-PCho pathway, with weaker capacity for the first methylation than PMT1 in some assays.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: bottleneck-associated but isoform-specific control not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes redundantly to phosphocholine production and viability, with minor individual growth effects but clear contribution in higher-order mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast complementation; recombinant enzyme kinetics; T-DNA mutants; double/triple mutant analysis; GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant phospho-base N-methyltransferase with tandem methyltransferase domains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Seedlings similar to PMT1, especially root tips; inflorescence stem; minor splice variant PMT2.2 enriched in flower buds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seedlings similar to PMT1, especially root tips; inflorescence stem; minor splice variant PMT2.2 enriched in flower buds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: salinity; cold in other plant PMTs discussed comparatively
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PMT2 is one of three Arabidopsis PMTs contributing to phosphocholine biosynthesis. 2. Single mutants show little morphology change, but combinations with pmt1 enhance short-root and severe growth phenotypes. 3. PMT2 splice-form-specific properties may influence tissue function and complementation ability.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PMT2.2 but not PMT2.1 rescued pmt1 short-root phenotype in promoter-swap experiments.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Sequentially methylates phosphobase intermediates to support phosphocholine production.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: bottleneck-associated but isoform-specific control not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major redundant PMT isoform affecting phosphocholine and choline levels; acts synergistically with PMT1 to sustain phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast complementation; recombinant enzyme kinetics; T-DNA mutants; double/triple mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant phospho-base N-methyltransferase with tandem methyltransferase domains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf veins, root vasculature/root tip, sepals, petals, anther filaments, ovules
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf veins, root vasculature/root tip, sepals, petals, anther filaments, ovules
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PMT3 contributes strongly to phosphocholine and choline pool maintenance despite lacking obvious single-mutant morphology. 2. Combined loss with PMT1 causes severe dwarfism and lowered PtdCho; triple loss abolishes de novo PtdCho biosynthesis. 3. PMT3 can functionally substitute for PMT1 in root complementation contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order mutant combinations cause dwarfism, aborted seeds/flowers and seedling lethality.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PMT3 single mutants reduced PCho and Cho to about half of wild type while PtdCho remained buffered.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes second and third methylation steps on phosphatidyl-base substrates but not the first methylation of phosphatidylethanolamine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PLMT1 has detectable phosphatidyl-base methylation capacity but does not make a major contribution to phosphatidylcholine accumulation in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: homology-based cloning; functional assay; mutant characterization; lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis PLMT1 can catalyze later phosphatidyl-base methylation steps. 2. atplmt mutants accumulate monomethyl and dimethyl PtdEtn. 3. Loss of PLMT1 does not alter PtdCho/PtdEtn levels or morphology, indicating a minor role in PtdCho biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes PMT rather than PLMT as the major route in seed plants.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts phosphocholine and CTP to CDP-choline.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point supported; historically postulated rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis in vivo and is activated by phosphatidic acid; altering its activity affects PtdCho accumulation in specific genetic contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; overexpression; recombinant enzyme assay; truncation analysis; lipid profiling in pah1 pah2 background
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CCT with C-terminal amphipathic lipid-binding domain
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCT1 catalyzes the phosphocholine-to-CDP-choline step in Arabidopsis. 2. Genetic evidence in the pah1 pah2 background supports its contribution to net PtdCho synthesis. 3. Its activity is stimulated by PA and modulated by a C-terminal lipid-binding domain.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phosphorylation at Ser-187 by SnRK1 reduced activity by ~70%; single mutants show limited lipid phenotypes, so double-mutant analysis remains needed.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts phosphocholine to CDP-choline in the CDP-choline pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to CCT activity and may function under low temperature, but its direct in vivo contribution to phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis remains less clear than CCT1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cloning; mutant analysis; activity assay; transcript/protein analysis under low temperature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCT2 is one of two Arabidopsis CCT isoforms for CDP-choline production. 2. Low temperature elevates CCT2 transcript/protein and activity, suggesting condition-specific regulation. 3. Direct lipid consequences of CCT2 loss are limited in single mutants, and its metabolic role remains incompletely resolved.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also implicated in basal immunity against P. syringae DC3000.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts phosphoethanolamine and CTP to CDP-ethanolamine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor shared by PtdEtn and indirectly PtdCho via common PEtn pool logic
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported by strong mutant phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for phosphatidylethanolamine biosynthesis and embryogenesis; reduced PECT1 decreases PtdEtn and often reciprocally increases PtdCho.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; TILLING mutants; genetic complementation; inducible expression; artificial microRNA suppression; lipid profiling; EYFP localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Emerging true leaves, shoot apices, stem vascular bundle, central region of pollen grain, globular-stage embryo
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Emerging true leaves, shoot apices, stem vascular bundle, central region of pollen grain, globular-stage embryo
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PECT1 is the Arabidopsis phosphoethanolamine cytidylyltransferase required for PE biosynthesis. 2. Null mutants are embryonic lethal, while partial-loss mutants are dwarfed and fertility-impaired, especially under low temperature. 3. Reduced PECT1 lowers PtdEtn and elevates PtdCho, indicating shared precursor partitioning between the two pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important leverage point for PE/PC ratio engineering, but strong developmental essentiality makes only partial modulation feasible.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Embryonic lethality and severe dwarfism when strongly reduced; altered flowering time in knockdown lines.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mitochondrial localization is emphasized as a notable unresolved feature.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers phosphocholine or phosphoethanolamine headgroups from CDP-activated intermediates to diacylglycerol to form phosphatidylcholine or phosphatidylethanolamine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions in the final assembly of phospholipids; genetic suppression reduces phospholipids and growth, while heterologous AAPT1 overexpression can increase PtdCho.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; RNAi suppression; heterologous overexpression (BnAAPT1 in Arabidopsis); lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAPT1 participates in the final transfer step producing PC/PE from CDP-headgroup intermediates and DAG. 2. Single Arabidopsis aapt1 mutants show mild phenotypes, implying redundancy with AAPT2. 3. Brassica napus AAPT1 overexpression in Arabidopsis increases PtdCho and improves low-temperature growth.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes plants likely use non-specific AAPTs rather than separate PC- and PE-specific enzymes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes terminal transfer of aminoalcohol phosphate headgroups to DAG to form PC or PE.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with AAPT1 in the terminal step of phospholipid synthesis; combined suppression affects pollen viability, seed set, phospholipid content and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; genetic interaction analysis; RNAi suppression; lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAPT2 is a second Arabidopsis AAPT isozyme functioning redundantly with AAPT1. 2. Single mutants are mild, but combined dosage reduction impairs pollen viability and seed development. 3. AAPT suppression lowers phospholipids, especially PE, supporting a major role in terminal phospholipid assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AAPT-independent compensation for PtdCho is suggested by RNAi lines.
